Class ReviewReply

java.lang.Object
application.ReviewReply
public class ReviewReply extends Object
Represents a reply to a review. Encapsulates the details of a reply, including its unique identifier, the ID of the review it responds to, the text content of the reply, the author of the reply, and the timestamp when the reply was created. Instances of this class are used to store and retrieve reply information from reviews in the database or to transfer reply data between layers of the application.

    The date and time when the reply was created.

    The unique identifier for this reply.
    private final String
    repliedBy
    The name or identifier of the user who created the reply.
    private final String
    replyText
    The textual content of the reply.
    private final int
    reviewId
    The identifier of the review to which this reply belongs.

  • Constructor Details

    • ReviewReply

      public ReviewReply(int id, int reviewId, String replyText, String repliedBy, LocalDateTime createdAt)
      Constructs for ReviewReply object
      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier for the reply
      reviewId - the identifier of the review being replied to
      replyText - the text content of the reply
      repliedBy - the user who authored the reply
      createdAt - the timestamp when the reply was created

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public int getId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this reply.
      Returns:
      the reply ID

    • getReviewId

      Returns the identifier of the review to which this reply belongs.
      Returns:
      the review ID

    • getReplyText

      public String getReplyText()
      Returns the textual content of the reply.
      Returns:
      the reply text, never null

      public String getRepliedBy()
      Returns the name or identifier of the user who created the reply.
      Returns:
      the author of the reply, never null

    • getCreatedAt

      public LocalDateTime getCreatedAt()
      Returns the date and time when the reply was created.
      Returns:
      the creation timestamp, never null
